narrative:
Step by step over the next four days, we break the grip of cold weather. This afternoon is 13–15 degrees warmer than Wednesday. Add another 8–10 degrees by Friday afternoon. Clouds increase later Friday night into Saturday, with a stray shower possible—especially on the west side of the mountain region. Then Sunday, high temperatures approach record levels, but that won’t last—cooler weather returns early next week.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Breaking Cold's Grip

Last Updated Thu March 19 at 11:01AM by Ray Russell

Step by step over the next four days, we break the grip of cold weather. This afternoon is 13–15 degrees warmer than Wednesday. Add another 8–10 degrees by Friday afternoon. Clouds increase later Friday night into Saturday, with a stray shower possible—especially on the west side of the mountain region. Then Sunday, high temperatures approach record levels, but that won’t last—cooler weather returns early next week.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

The deep trough carved into the Eastern U.S. gradually retreats and shifts eastward. At the surface, high pressure moves from the East Coast to the Gulf of Mexico, producing a warmer southerly flow.

Skies remain variably cloudy through Friday. Temperatures gradually become milder—seasonal today and 8–10 degrees above average Friday.

A weak disturbance adds clouds Friday night into Saturday morning. A stray shower or two is possible late Friday night into Saturday morning, especially on the west side of the Appalachians.

We get the full surge of warm air Sunday, as temperatures approach records for the date.

A front moves through from the north late Sunday night. Clouds increase, and a few showers develop late Sunday night into Monday morning. Monday night and Tuesday, a cool wedge develops down the east side of the Appalachians. We feel the cool-down Monday afternoon and Tuesday. The cool-down is not extreme, just taking us back to seasonal temperatures. The wedge lingers into Wednesday with a stray shower again possible. The wedge scours out later Wednesday into Thursday.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for March 16, 2026

Quiet weather returns for western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia after a hyper-active Monday around the region. Severe weather is followed by a sharp cold shot and mountain snow shower episode, then we moderate nicely for late week through the weekend.

Mar 16, 2026
Mid-March Madness

A strong cold front crosses the Southern Appalachians Monday morning and moves into the Foothills Monday afternoon.

Rain and thunderstorms accompany the frontal passage, and severe thunderstorms are ...

Mar 16, 2026
Severe Weather Season Around The Corner

As our March weather has become more changeable and volatile, it's a reminder that severe weather season is almost upon us.

Last week was Severe Weather Awareness Week in North Carolina; This week is Virginia's turn.

While western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia may have less overall severe weather than our surrounding neighbors, we still have our fair share, and it's a great time to review your severe weather emergency/preparedeness procedures ahead of time.
